generated_oml:
// C3: Physical-to-virtual interaction (DataTransmitted)
instance SensorDataTraceAggregator : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Aggregates contextual data traces from heterogeneous physical sources (e.g., PLCs, sensors, edge devices) with event-triggered or periodic transmission mechanisms. Includes preprocessing steps like filtering, normalization, or dimensionality reduction."
]

instance OperationalLogDataTrace : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Collects operational logs and system telemetry from physical components, structured for alignment with digital shadow architecture."
]

instance PhysicalStateDataTrace : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Transmits real-time state data from physical system components, serving as input for Digital Shadow's passive data aggregation layer."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance DigitalShadowReferenceModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Defines the foundational data aggregation layer for Digital Shadow, structured as a UML class diagram. Serves as the semantic backbone for contextual data traces."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalShadowDataManager
]

instance SysMLFunctionalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML-based functional architecture model capturing system requirements, functional/logical architecture, and product layer decomposition."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLIntegrationEnabler
]

instance MontiArcComponentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MontiArc-based component model defining interfaces, interconnections, and compositions with behavioral extensions via MontiArc Automaton."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcSimulationEnabler
]

instance SimulinkBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink model for detailed dynamic system simulation, integrated with SysML for end-to-end behavioral analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance CppControlLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "General-purpose C++ implementation for control logic and custom algorithms, coupled with simulation models."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlLogicExecutionEnabler
]

instance AggregatedContextualData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed and contextualized data traces derived from raw sensor/operational logs, structured for Digital Twin services."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SensorDataTraceAggregator, OperationalLogDataTrace, PhysicalStateDataTrace
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalShadowDataManager, DataAbstractionEnabler
]

instance HistoricalOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series historical data from physical system operations, used for trend analysis and predictive modeling."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperationalLogDataTrace
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler
]

instance RealTimeSystemStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Near-real-time state data from physical components, correlated with digital representations for monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:fromData PhysicalStateDataTrace
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MonitoringServiceEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DigitalShadowDataManager : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Manages data aggregation, abstraction, and contextualization as per Digital Shadow Reference Model. Implements filtering/normalization pipelines."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataInterpretationService, MonitoringService
]

instance SysMLIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Facilitates integration between SysML models (IBDs, state machines) and other tools via model transformation (e.g., ATL, QVT)."
    DTDFVocab:enables ArchitectureValidationService
]

instance MontiArcSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Executes MontiArc Automaton-based behavioral models and coordinates with simulation tools."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveSimulationService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Runs MathWorks Simulink simulations for dynamic system behavior analysis, integrated with SysML models."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveSimulationService
]

instance ControlLogicExecutionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Executes C++/Java-based control logic and custom algorithms for system automation."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlAutomationService
]

instance DataAbstractionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Performs data abstraction and dimensionality reduction on raw traces to derive actionable insights."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataInterpretationService
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Leverages simulation tools and historical data for behavioral analysis and predictive modeling."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveOptimizationService
]

instance MonitoringServiceEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Correlates real-time physical and digital states for anomaly detection and monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataInterpretationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Acts as active intermediary processing one-way data flows from Digital Shadow into actionable insights via aggregation and contextual analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides ProcessedDataInsight, DataAbstractionAction
]

instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables real-time/near-real-time state monitoring with anomaly detection via pattern recognition in aggregated data traces."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStateInsight, AnomalyDetectionAction
]

instance PredictiveSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Models future system states using simulation tools (Simulink, MontiArc) based on current data."
    DTDFVocab:provides FutureStateInsight, SimulationRecommendationAction
]

instance PredictiveOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides optimization recommendations (process adjustments, resource allocation) using behavioral models."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationInsight, ProcessAdjustmentAction
]

instance ControlAutomationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Implements closed-loop integration with physical actuators via C++/Java control logic (implied from active processing role)."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlSignalAction
]

instance ArchitectureValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Validates model alignment between physical system and digital representation using SysML/ADL methodologies."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelValidationInsight
]

instance VisualizationDecisionSupportService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Delivers interactive visualizations of system states for stakeholder interpretation and decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizedSystemStateInsight, DecisionSupportAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ProcessedDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Aggregated and contextually analyzed data traces ready for higher-level processing."
]

instance SystemStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Correlated physical-digital state representation with anomaly detection patterns."
]

instance FutureStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted system states derived from simulation models based on current operational data."
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Process adjustments and resource allocation recommendations from behavioral optimization."
]

instance ModelValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation results comparing digital model fidelity against physical system behavior."
]

instance VisualizedSystemStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive visual representation of system states for stakeholder interpretation."
]

instance DataAbstractionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated data abstraction and dimensionality reduction of raw traces."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AnomalyDetectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated pattern recognition for anomaly detection in system data traces."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SimulationRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated generation of simulation-based recommendations for system adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ProcessAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated implementation of process adjustments based on optimization insights."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlSignalAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated generation of control signals for physical actuators."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DecisionSupportAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Provides contextualized insights for stakeholder decision-making (manual process)."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** is implicitly defined through the **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** framework, which employs **multi-notation modeling** (e.g., SysML for architecture, Simulink for behavior, and general-purpose languages like C++/Java for implementation). Orchestration and coordination rely on **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as ACME, AADL, or MontiArc, which define **component integration** via **interfaces, connectors, and composition rules**. Resource management is not explicitly detailed, but the document suggests **heterogeneous tool integration** (e.g., simulation tools, code) to handle system-wide coordination. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Fig. 1) implies a **passive data aggregation** approach, contrasting with DTs, which require **active components** for data interpretation and processing. The document does not specify orchestration patterns (e.g., microservices, service-oriented) but emphasizes the need to integrate **multiple modeling notations and tools** for comprehensive system-wide coordination."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The lifecycle stages of a Digital Twin (DT) system, as inferred from the provided context, are not explicitly detailed. However, the document implies a structured modeling approach aligned with **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, which typically include the following phases: **requirements definition**, **functional architecture design**, **logical architecture modeling**, and **product/technical layer implementation**. These phases are often managed using **multi-abstraction methodologies** such as **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)**, **OOSEM (Object-Oriented Systems Engineering Method)**, **SPES (Systems Engineering Process for Embedded Systems)**, or **SMArDT (Smart Digital Twin)**. The transition between phases involves **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **ACME**, **AADL**, **MontiArc**, or **SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)** to define components, interfaces, interconnections, and composition. Behavioral modeling is further refined using tools like **MathWorks Simulink**, other simulation frameworks (e.g., [29], [30]), or general-purpose programming languages (e.g., **C++**, **Java**). Lifecycle governance is implicitly tied to **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** and **multi-notation model consistency**, though no specific governance framework (e.g., ISO 15288, CMMI) is mentioned in the provided documents. The document does not specify lifecycle representation types (e.g., virtual, physical, hybrid) or explicit transition management strategies beyond the use of ADLs and MBSE tools."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system incorporates **fidelity and validity considerations** through **validation and verification (V&V) methods** aligned with MBSE practices. Model fidelity is ensured via **multi-layered abstraction** (e.g., requirements, functional, logical, and physical layers) and **methodologies like RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)** or **SMArDT**, which systematically refine models from high-level specifications to executable implementations. **Uncertainty sources** are addressed through **data aggregation and abstraction** (as per the Digital Shadow Reference Model [32]), where contextual traces are processed to mitigate noise and inconsistencies. **Error detection and correction mechanisms** are implied via the use of **simulation tools (e.g., Simulink)** and **ADLs (e.g., MontiArc Automaton, SysML state machines)** to cross-validate behavioral models against physical system dynamics. **Quality assurance processes** involve **stakeholder integration** and **heterogeneous tool interoperability** to ensure traceability and consistency across models [31]."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The document does not explicitly address **horizontal integration** with external systems, including **data exchange protocols, interoperability standards, or dependencies on external systems**. However, it implies **integration patterns** through the use of **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM, SPES) and **ADLs** (e.g., SysML, MontiArc), which support **heterogeneous stakeholder perspectives** and **cross-system dependencies**. The document does not mention **specific protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, REST) or **enterprise system integrations** (e.g., ERP, MES), nor does it detail **Digital Twin-to-Digital Twin (DT2DT) interoperability**. The focus is on **internal system modeling** rather than external system connectivity."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The document does not provide explicit details on **standards compliance**, **certification requirements**, or **interoperability frameworks** for Digital Twins (DTs). However, it references **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies and **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **SysML** ([17]), **ACME**, **AADL**, **MontiArc**, and **SMArDT**, which are aligned with broader **systems engineering standards** (e.g., **ISO 15288**, **IEC 62278**). The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Fig. 1) implies a **UML-based class diagram** for manufacturing contexts, suggesting adherence to **UML 2.x** or **SysML** standards. No **industry-specific standards** (e.g., **IIoT, RAMI 4.0, NIST DT Framework**) or **certification bodies** (e.g., **ISO/IEC 17024**) are mentioned. **Interoperability** is indirectly addressed through the need to integrate **heterogeneous modeling notations** and tools (e.g., ADLs + simulation frameworks), but no explicit protocols (e.g., **OPC UA**, **FDT/DTM**) are referenced."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the MABI MAX 100 serial robot incorporates **safety-critical system considerations** through the integration of a **safety cell** that restricts the operational range of the first vertical axis to **[-90°, 90°]**, ensuring physical constraints are enforced. **Threat mitigation strategies** are implicitly addressed via the **dual-encoder sensor arrangement** (motor encoder and secondary encoder behind the gearbox), which reduces **backlash** and **positional uncertainty** by cross-referencing encoder data to infer **elasticity and friction** in the drive train. The **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller**, used for axis control, supports **fail-safe mechanisms** by enabling separate control of axes with configurable inputs (position, speed, acceleration) and outputs (target/actual position, derivatives, motor current). **Access control mechanisms** are not explicitly detailed, but the **closed-loop control system** (via motor current-to-torque conversion using known motor constants) ensures deterministic behavior critical for safety. **Risk assessment** is implied through the system’s reliance on **high-precision encoders** (reducing uncertainty to sub-millimeter levels) and **gear transmission design** (optimized for torque output and speed constraints). No explicit mention of **cybersecurity protocols**, **authentication systems**, or **data protection regulations** is provided in the document."
]
